title: Adding virtio-serial device
body:
### Issue Description

## Description
While start machine, status is keeping Currently starting and start vm was stopped at  **Adding virtio-serial device**

```
ec2-user@Mac-mini testcontainer % podman machine  list
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED        LAST UP             CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  applehv     9 minutes ago  Currently starting  4           2GiB        50GiB
```

When run with debugging, procedure is **stopped "Adding virtio-serial device"**.
Could you advise **why start machine was stopped at Adding virtio-serial device**?

```
ec2-user@Mac-mini testcontainer % podman --debug machine start
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider

Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

```


## Environment
```
 % sw_vers
ProductName:            macOS
ProductVersion:         15.0
BuildVersion:           24A335

ec2-user@Mac-mini testcontainer % podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49846: connect: connection refused
ec2-user@Mac-mini testcontainer % podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49846: connect: connection refused
```

comments.body: consider trying to do `podman --log-level debug machine start` and it should pop a GUI window of the boot.  Perhaps it will reveal whats going on?

After restart MacOS, error was disappare. I can't reproduce error and want to close issue.
Thanks for comment.
